topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The knock on the door sounded like a gunshot in the quiet apartment. I froze mid-sip of my lukewarm tea, my hand hovering over the mug as if it were a live wire. A delivery person stood on the stoop, holding a plain brown box addressed to the previous occupant. My stomach tightened, a cold knot forming just beneath my ribs. Who would be sending something here? I slowly lowered the mug, the clink of ceramic against the saucer sounding deafening in the silence. Every nerve ending felt hyper-alert, scanning the hallway, the windows, the shadows pooling in the corners of the room. I didn't move toward the door. I just stared, my breath catching in my throat, waiting for the next move.

[story 2]

It was the sound of the mail slot clattering, a sharp, unwelcome intrusion into the late afternoon lull. I shuffled to the entryway, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. The package was there, sitting squarely on the welcome mat—a medium-sized, unmarked carton. My knuckles were white as I gripped the edge of the doorframe. My heart hammered against my sternum, a frantic rhythm I couldn't slow down. I peered through the peephole, my face draining of color as I saw the delivery driver lingering just outside the frame. I mumbled something incoherent, my voice raspy, and retreated back into the living room, sinking onto the sofa as if gravity had suddenly increased tenfold.

[story 3]

The doorbell chimed, a cheerful, jarring sound that scraped against the strained quiet of the evening. I stood by the kitchen counter, my posture rigid, every muscle coiled tight. When I opened the door, the package was immediately visible, sitting on the threshold like an accusation. I didn't speak; I simply took the box, my fingers brushing against the rough cardboard as if it were contaminated. A wave of profound unease washed over me, making the air feel thick and hard to breathe. I backed away slowly, my eyes darting toward the hallway, searching for an exit, any escape route from the sudden, suffocating awareness that something unwelcome had just crossed the threshold into my space.
